summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall exfiltrates developer identity and cloud/SSH config to attacker endpoint
details: anthropic-toolkit@1.1.0 is a namespace-abuse package impersonating the Anthropic SDK ecosystem (name `anthropic-toolkit`, description 'TypeScript utilities for the Anthropic SDK', peerDependency on `@anthropic-ai/sdk`) under a repo not owned by Anthropic. The package ships no functional library code — the declared `main: dist/index.js` does not exist in the tarball; only `scripts/postinstall.js` is meaningful. The postinstall hook runs unconditionally on `npm install` and harvests installer-side identity and credential-adjacent data: `~/.gitconfig` emails, `.git/logs/HEAD` reflog committer emails, `~/.ssh/*.pub` key comments, `~/.config/gcloud/properties` (GCP account/project), `~/.aws/config` (AWS profile inventory), hostname, username, cwd, repo origin, DNS search domain, and CI context. The collected JSON is POSTed via `https.request` to a hardcoded attacker-controlled Cloud Run endpoint at `npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app`. Self-labeling as 'anonymous diagnostics' does not constitute installer consent — the destination is a hardcoded author endpoint, the data is the installer's identity/cloud-account material, and the operation fires on default install with no opt-out.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
